note: Core-centric theorem -> 01e-property mapping (Stage C). Per the gasper-lean4 maintainer (fab/banri): ~70-80% of the proved substance lives in GasperBeaconChain.Core.* (Theories + Lemmas), NOT the thin, still-growing Executable application layer. So the primary targets are the Core Theories/Lemmas (top-level accountable safety k_safety', the slashable bound, plausible liveness, and the justification/quorum lemmas). The Executable.* entries are retained but reframed as the DECIDABLE/COMPUTABLE Bool-checker versions of the corresponding Core theorem -- that is their real role. NOTE on names: every Core declaration lives in the flat `GasperBeaconChain.Core` namespace regardless of its source subdirectory (Theories/ and Lemmas/ are file paths, not namespace segments), so the fully-qualified `theorem` is `GasperBeaconChain.Core.<name>`; the source path is recorded in x_layer. Fields prefixed x_ are documentation (incl. x_stride, the STRIDE class fab requested) and are NOT emitted; the emitted `type` follows the benchmark vocabulary (invariant).

properties.text: Two conflicting k-finalized blocks must force a slashable two-thirds quorum intersection (top-level k-accountable safety)
properties.assertion: k_finalized(b1) and k_finalized(b2) and not_ancestor(b1,b2) and not_ancestor(b2,b1) -> q_intersection_slashed(st)
properties.x_exploitability_note: The headline safety result: any two conflicting finalized histories are provably attributable to a slashable two-thirds intersection. A client that finalizes a fork without this attribution invariant breaks accountable safety.

properties.text: A one-finalization fork must be exactly a same-height k-finalization fork at k=1
properties.assertion: finalization_fork(st) <-> same_k_finalization_fork(st, k=1)
properties.x_exploitability_note: Structural bridge equating the 1-finalization fork condition with the general k-finalization fork at k=1; a client conflating the two mis-detects fork conditions.

properties.text: Absent slashing, no justified block may share the height of a distinct k-finalized block
properties.assertion: k_finalized(bf,h1) and justified(bj,h2) and not slashed and bj!=bf -> h2 != h1
properties.x_exploitability_note: Height-uniqueness of finalized vs justified checkpoints given no slashing; violated only if the client accepts a conflicting justification at a finalized height.

properties.text: A justification link surrounding a k-finalized block must force a slashable quorum intersection (surround case)
properties.assertion: justified(s) and link(s,t) and k_finalized(final) and final_h<t_h and s_h<final_h and not_ancestor(final,t) -> q_intersection_slashed(st)
properties.x_exploitability_note: The surround-vote branch of the accountable-safety case split: a link straddling a finalized block is slashable. Missing this lets surround voting finalize conflicts undetected.

properties.text: Two conflicting k-finalized blocks at different heights must force a slashable quorum intersection
properties.assertion: k_finalized(b1,h1) and k_finalized(b2,h2) and h2<h1 and not_ancestor(b2,b1) -> q_intersection_slashed(st)
properties.x_exploitability_note: The unequal-height branch of accountable safety; combined with the equal-height case it yields k_safety'.

properties.text: A conflicting k-finalization must exhibit two quorums whose intersection weight meets the churn-adjusted slashable lower bound
properties.assertion: conflicting_k_finalization(st) -> exists qL,qR: wt(qL inter qR) >= max(wt(vL)-act-ext, wt(vR)-act-ext)
properties.x_exploitability_note: The quantitative half of accountable safety: the slashable intersection has provably positive, churn-adjusted weight. A client whose effective-balance / weight arithmetic diverges from this bound miscomputes how much stake is slashable and whether the 2/3 threshold is met.

properties.text: Two two-thirds quorums must intersect in weight at least their combined weight minus the one-third thresholds
properties.assertion: 2/3-quorum(qL,vL) and 2/3-quorum(qR,vR) -> wt(vL inter vR) - 1/3wt(vL) - 1/3wt(vR) <= wt(qL inter qR)
properties.x_exploitability_note: Inclusion-exclusion lower bound on quorum overlap. Weight arithmetic that overflows or double-counts churned validators can misjudge a two-thirds threshold.

properties.text: Validator-set intersection weight must be bounded below by a churn-adjusted (activation/exit) expression
properties.assertion: max(wt(vL)-actwt(v0,vL)-extwt(v0,vR), wt(vR)-actwt(v0,vR)-extwt(v0,vL)) <= wt(vL inter vR)
properties.x_exploitability_note: Bounds the overlap of two validator sets accounting for activation/exit churn relative to a reference set; the arithmetic backbone of the slashable bound. A divergent effective-balance computation violates it.

properties.text: Absent slashing, two distinct blocks must never both be justified at the same height
properties.assertion: justified(b1,h1) and justified(b2,h2) and not slashed and b1!=b2 -> h1 != h2
properties.x_exploitability_note: No two distinct justified blocks share a height without slashing; the structural core of same-height safety.

properties.text: Two distinct blocks justified at the same height must force a slashable quorum intersection
properties.assertion: justified(b1,h) and justified(b2,h) and b1!=b2 -> q_intersection_slashed(st)
properties.x_exploitability_note: The converse witness: same-height justification is provably slashable. Missing this detection permits equivocation to justify conflicts.

properties.text: A finalized block must have a justified child at the next height
properties.assertion: finalized(p,h) -> exists c: parent(p,c) and justified(c, h+1)
properties.x_exploitability_note: Finalization implies a justified child; an off-by-one in the finalize/justify transition breaks this.

properties.text: A k-finalized block must itself be justified
properties.assertion: k_finalized(b,h,k) -> justified(b,h)
properties.x_exploitability_note: k-finalization entails justification; a client treating finalized-but-not-justified states as valid violates it.

properties.text: The plain finalized predicate must coincide exactly with one-finalization (k=1)
properties.assertion: finalized(b,h) <-> k_finalized(b,h,1)
properties.x_exploitability_note: Bridges plain finalization and the k-generalized definition at k=1; a mismatch means the two finalization code paths disagree.

properties.text: Any superset of a two-thirds quorum within the validator set must itself be a two-thirds quorum
properties.assertion: q subset q' and q' subset vset(b) and quorum_2(q,b) -> quorum_2(q',b)
properties.x_exploitability_note: Up-closure of the two-thirds quorum predicate; monotonicity a client's quorum test must respect.

properties.text: A two-thirds quorum must be non-empty whenever the two-thirds weight threshold is positive
properties.assertion: 0 < two_third(wt(vset(b))) and quorum_2(q,b) -> q.Nonempty
properties.x_exploitability_note: A positive threshold forces a non-empty quorum; an empty-quorum acceptance (e.g. zero-weight threshold bug) violates it.

properties.text: With two-thirds honest stake and no slashing the state must be extendable to finalize a new checkpoint without new slashing
properties.assertion: two_thirds_good(st) and not slashed and blocks_exist_high -> exists st': unslashed_can_extend(st,st') and no_new_slashed(st,st') and finalizes_new(st')
properties.x_exploitability_note: Plausible liveness by explicit two-link extension. Liveness property; typically outside a client bug-bounty's safety scope.

properties.text: Plausible liveness must hold under the Coq-style block-existence hypothesis for the highest justified block
properties.assertion: two_thirds_good(st) and not slashed and blocks_exist_high_coq -> exists st': unslashed_can_extend(st,st') and no_new_slashed(st,st') and finalizes_new(st')
properties.x_exploitability_note: The plausible-liveness result restated against the Coq block-existence predicate (interoperability with the reference Coq model). Liveness; out of safety scope.

properties.text: Extending the state with two supermajority-link vote batches must introduce no new slashing
properties.assertion: highest_justified(s1) and good_votes and quorum_2(q1,t1) and quorum_2(q2,t2) and s1_h<=H -> no_new_slashed(st, extend_two_links(st))
properties.x_exploitability_note: The no-new-slashing guarantee that makes the liveness extension honest; underpins plausible liveness. Liveness; out of safety scope.

properties.text: The decidable double-vote checker must flag exactly the validators that signed two distinct targets at one target epoch (S1)
properties.assertion: slashedB_S1(st,v) <-> exists two attestations by v with same target_epoch and distinct target_root
properties.x_exploitability_note: Computable Bool checker (iff a bounded existential) for Casper condition S1. A client whose detection disagrees with this predicate misses equivocation.

properties.text: The decidable surround-vote checker must flag exactly the validators with a surrounding source-target attestation pair (S2)
properties.assertion: slashedB_S2(st,v) <-> exists a1,a2 by v with source(a1)<source(a2) and target(a2)<target(a1)
properties.x_exploitability_note: Computable Bool checker (iff a bounded existential) for Casper condition S2. A client whose detection disagrees permits surround voting.

properties.text: The decidable accountable-safety witness must certify a slashable quorum intersection for any one-finalization fork
properties.assertion: finalization_fork(st) -> witnessB(st) constructs a q_intersection_slashed witness
properties.x_exploitability_note: The computable witness form of accountable safety (Core.accountable_safety); lets a client actually produce the slashable intersection rather than only assert it exists.

properties.text: The decidable k-accountable-safety witness must certify a slashable quorum intersection for any k-finalization fork
properties.assertion: k_finalization_fork(st,k1,k2) -> k_witnessB(st) constructs a q_intersection_slashed witness
properties.x_exploitability_note: Computable witness form of k_safety' under dynamic validator sets; the executable counterpart of the headline Core theorem.

properties.x_core_counterpart: decidable form of the q_intersection_slashed predicate used across Core
properties.text: The decidable quorum-intersection-slashed check must hold iff a concrete slashing witness exists
properties.assertion: qIntersectionWitnessB(st) <-> exists witness w: q_intersection_slashed via w
properties.x_exploitability_note: Makes the q_intersection_slashed predicate (the conclusion of every accountable-safety theorem) decidable by an explicit witness search.

properties.text: The decidable justification check must match a bounded supermajority-link chain from the anchor with no unbounded recursion
properties.assertion: justified(c) <-> exists bounded chain of supermajority links from anchor to c (decNthAncestor bounded)
properties.x_exploitability_note: Decidable, bounded characterization of justification; an unbounded slot/ancestor iterator diverges from it (off-by-one on the height gap or non-termination).

properties.x_core_counterpart: decidable form used by Core plausible-liveness hypotheses
properties.text: The decidable two-thirds-honest check must hold iff every target admits a good quorum
properties.assertion: two_thirds_good(st) <-> forall target, exists good quorum at that target (goodQuorumAtB)
properties.x_exploitability_note: Decidable form of the two-thirds-honest hypothesis that plausible liveness needs. Liveness; out of safety scope.
